title: Mikapika's 3D Mushrooms
description: A resource pack that makes placed mushrooms 3D.
body:
![A screenshot of the game Minecraft. Taken within the Overworld dimension, in the Old Growth Pine Taiga biome. The camera is focused at the ground, and the brown mushrooms dotting the podzol among ferns.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/fd29397a4a8395db43a876598bab2047b485fec4.webp)
## About

I was bored and decided to create a resource pack about it.

This resource pack makes mushrooms appear three-dimensional when placed on blocks or in a flower pot. I wanted to make them 3D when placed down, because I feel it suits them better. I'm not a massive fan of making _everything_ 3D, but when used sparingly I think it does much better than a cross texture.

![A screenshot from the game Minecraft. Overlooking a row of four flower pots placed on top of smooth tuff blocks. From left to right, in each pot is a brown mushroom, a red mushrrom, a warped fungus, and a crimson fungus respectively.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/216cc2291517cd534fd9ea18d8b4a14035f75bdf.webp)

I have also created sprites for the inventory textures. A gripe I have is that most of the textures of flora in the inventory are just the sprite that is used for the cross texture when placed. I wish textures for items in containers were unique, that way they could be more stylised, and better convey what the item is. I myself cannot be bothered doing that.

![A screenshot of the game Minecraft. Taken within the Nether dimension, in a Crimson Forest biome. Dotted about the scene are five crimson mushrooms.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/0230f793936c7d128233dbca3088c5b3ac42fe9e.webp)

A small detail I used on the nether fungi was an "outer layer" similar to what a skin has. I think these small layers are really good at giving even more depth to a model without being as jarring as a full voxel.

Shaders used in screenshots: EminGT's _Complementary Shaders - Reimagined ([_link_](https://modrinth.com/shader/complementary-reimagined))_

_NOTE: Game versions below 1.20.2 will display this pack as unsupported due to the 'supported_formats' property not being introduced until then. The pack will, in fact, work down to version 1.13._
